# Performance Hot Path

The common case must stay cheap.

For HydraCache, the common case is a local cache hit:

```text
logical key -> cache lookup -> decode -> return
```

Everything else should pay its own cost:

- loader execution
- tag invalidation
- metrics
- distributed synchronization
- DB adapter work

```{mermaid}
%%| label: fig-hot-path-boundary
%%| fig-cap: "Keep optional work out of the local hit path."
flowchart LR
  A[Cache Hit] --> B[Moka Lookup]
  B --> C[Decode]
  C --> D[Return]
  E[Invalidation] -.outside hot path.-> B
  F[Cluster Sync] -.outside hot path.-> B
  G[DB Adapter] -.miss path only.-> B
```

## Source Notes

Use these files when expanding this chapter:

- [Performance hot path note](../learning/performance-hot-path.md)
- [Moka reread](../../../moka/MOKA_HYDRACACHE_REREAD.md)
- [Caffeine reread](../../../caffeine/CAFFEINE_HYDRACACHE_REREAD.md)
- [HikariCP reread](../../../hikaricp/HIKARICP_HYDRACACHE_REREAD.md)
